title: Stack picks docker-compose.yaml over compose.yaml unpredictably
body: When a stack directory has both a legacy `docker-compose.yaml` and a new `compose.yaml`, the Stack constructor in stack.ts iterates `acceptedComposeFileNames` and takes the first match, but the array order isn't documented, so it's unclear (and seemingly inconsistent across stacks) which file actually gets edited/deployed from the UI.

title: Document supported steps for running Dockge against MySQL
body: database.ts hardcodes a good bit of SQLite-specific setup (the @louislam/sqlite3 dialect patch, knexMigrationsPath, etc.) but DBConfig also has a 'mysql' type option. Is MySQL actually a supported production backend, and if so what's the migration/setup path from the default SQLite install?

title: Terminal output buffer (LimitQueue of 100) drops early compose pull logs
body: For stacks with a lot of image layers to pull, terminal.ts's LimitQueue(100) buffer can already be full of pull progress lines by the time the frontend socket attaches and requests scrollback, so the very beginning of the `docker compose up` output is gone from the UI even though the container is still running fine.

title: Login rate limiting resets on every server restart
body: rate-limiter.ts appears to track login attempts in memory only, so restarting the Dockge process (e.g. after an update) resets all rate-limit counters, giving an attacker a fresh window right after every restart. Should this be persisted, or at least documented as an accepted limitation?

title: primaryHostname fallback to localhost is confusing in multi-agent setups
body: When Settings 'primaryHostname' isn't configured, Stack.toJSON() falls back to parsing the request endpoint's hostname, or 'localhost' if that fails. In a multi-agent deployment behind a reverse proxy this sometimes shows 'localhost' links in the UI for a stack actually running on a remote agent. Could agent-manager.ts surface the agent's own hostname as a better fallback?
